pages.title: Lighting in Three.js
pages.body:
# Lighting in Three.js

The preview cycles through five lighting modes. Each mode uses different light types to demonstrate a distinct look.

Artlab exposes light factories on ctx:

```
ambient(color, intensity)
point(color, intensity, distance, decay)
directional(color, intensity)
spot(color, intensity, distance, angle)
hemi(skyColor, groundColor, intensity)
```

Lights added with ctx.add() affect every MeshStandardMaterial object in the scene. MeshBasicMaterial ignores lights entirely.

pages.title: Ambient Light
pages.body:
# Ambient Light

Ambient light fills the entire scene uniformly — no direction, no shadows.

```
const fill = ctx.ambient(0x888888, 0.4);
ctx.add(fill);
```

Alone it produces flat, directionless shading. Use it as a minimum floor so objects never go completely black in shadow.

## Good Practice

Keep ambient intensity low (0.1–0.4) and use directional or point lights for the primary illumination. High ambient destroys depth cues and makes scenes look washed out.
